channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Orlando and Chicago square off for conference showdown
item.description: Chicago Bulls (1-0, fourth in the Eastern Conference) vs. Orlando Magic (1-1, eighth in the Eastern Conference) Orlando, Florida; Saturday, 6 p.m. CDT BETMGM SPORTSBOOK LINE: Magic -5.5; over/under is 232.5 BOTTOM LINE: Chicago takes on Orlando for an Eastern Conference matchup.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Serra pulls away from Mitty as Jeovanni Henley atones for fumble with a pick-six
item.description: Jeovanni Henely, a two-way star for Serra, made a name for himself Friday night against Mitty after turning a miscue into a momentum-flipping, highlight-reel play
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Barger, Varsho and Kirk lead homer barrage as Blue Jays rout Dodgers 11-4 in World Series opener
item.description: Addison Barger launched the first pinch-hit grand slam in World Series history, Alejandro Kirk added a two-run homer in a nine-run sixth inning and the Toronto Blue Jays overwhelmed the defending champion Los Angeles Dodgers 11-4 in Game 1.
item.dc:creator: Ronald Blum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Terry Rozier's attorney accuses FBI of seeking 'misplaced glory' with NBA player's arrest
item.description: Miami Heat's Terry Rozier was arrested by the FBI as part of a sports betting probe. The NBA player faces charges after allegedly rigging prop bets in 2023 game.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alleged mob ties in NBA scandal recall La Cosa Nostra’s long shadow over sports
item.description: NBA coach Chauncey Billups and player Terry Rozier were arrested in a massive illegal gambling scheme involving organized crime families and rigged poker games, the FBI said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NBA coach Billups, Heat's Rozier arrested in FBI gambling probe
item.description: More than 30 people were arrested in connection with a federal probe into sports gambling and rigged poker games, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Vucevic, Buzelis, Giddey lead way, Bulls beat Pistons 115-111 after blowing big lead
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Nikola Vucevic had 28 points and 14 rebounds, Matas Buzelis scored 21 points and the Chicago Bulls regrouped after blowing a 23-point lead to beat the Detroit Pistons 115-111 in their opener Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bad Bunny's Super Bowl halftime show is not being reconsidered: NFL commissioner
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The NFL is not considering dropping Bad Bunny as its Super Bowl halftime headline performer, Commissioner Roger Goodell said Wednesday, reaffirming a decision to put the Grammy-winning Puerto Rican artist on the league's biggest stage that led to criticism from U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mammoth beat Colorado in overtime, 4-3, for fourth straight win
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Mammoth's first homestand of the season could not have gone much better. Dylan Guenther scored the game-winning goal 33 seconds into overtime as the Mammoth beat the Colorado Avalanche, 4-3, to improve to 4-0 at the Delta Center this season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Former NFL player who died in police custody battled injuries and mental health challenges
item.description: Martin, 36, died after what police described as a “brief struggle” with officers who arrested him early Saturday while investigating a break-in at a home. He became unresponsive and was pronounced dead at a hospital, Oakland police said. A cause of death has not been released.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and MARK LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Women's pro baseball league picks San Francisco for new team
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Women's professional sports are thriving in San Francisco. The city just marked its first season with a WNBA team, the Golden State Valkyries. Starting next year, San Francisco Giants fans will have another home team to root for.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KRON4

item.title: Former NFL player Doug Martin died after struggling with officers while being detained, police say
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Former All-Pro NFL running back Doug Martin died after a struggle with police officers who were taking him into custody while they were investigating a break-in at a home in Oakland, police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: BYU beats Utah for third straight time, 24-21
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU rules the rivalry. The Cougars extended its winning streak over arch-rival Utah to three straight games with a 24-21 victory over the Utes Saturday night at LaVell Edwards Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Pickleball eye injuries on the rise as sport gains popularity: Study
item.description: A study published in JAMA Ophthalmology on Thursday suggests that "pickleball-related eye injuries have increased at an alarming rate over the past 4 years,"
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation
